own_review.issue: KEY DEFECT: the per-directory startsWith guards (`executable.startsWith(binDirectory)` / `command[0].startsWith(binDirectory + "/")`) only skip the matching directory's iteration. If the command is already an absolute path under one bin dir (e.g. /usr/local/bin/docker), the loop still prepends the OTHER directory, producing a garbage path like /opt/homebrew/bin//usr/local/bin/docker. The old single-guard code in ProcessRunner/Condition skipped the fallback entirely in that case. The guards are also unnecessary since commands never contain '/' in practice. Correct fix is a single startsWith("/") absolute-path guard (as done in polish); the human reviewer asked to revert all three guards.

Mutation aliasing: processBuilder.command(command) mutates the shared builder inside the loop; the second iteration copies the already-mutated list and only works because index 0 is overwritten from the pre-captured `executable`. Fragile, and leaves the caller's ProcessBuilder mutated when all attempts fail.

catch (Exception ignored) swallows fallback failure details entirely (including InterruptedException without restoring interrupt status); the final RuntimeException carries neither cause nor suppressed exceptions, hurting diagnosability.

Fallback no longer routes through startProcess(), duplicating the directory(workingDirectory) setup; final ProcessStartException now references the original command with suppressed fallback errors (behavior change, arguably an improvement).
Pre-existing inconsistencies preserved: CredentialHelper has no PATH-containment guard while the other two do; trailing-slash convention in the constants differs across the three classes.

points.point: CredentialHelper.java:100-112 fragile mutation-in-loop pattern; works accidentally because index 0 is overwritten; suggests capturing original command or fresh ProcessBuilder
points.note: Verified real and the analysis is exactly correct.

qualitative: The decisive defect is the per-directory startsWith guards: for a command already rooted in one bin dir (or any absolute path), the loop prepends the other dir, yielding nonsense like /opt/homebrew/bin//usr/local/bin/docker — the human asked to revert all three guards, and the polish fix replaced them with a startsWith("/") check. v4 missed this entirely and even declared the PR 'functionally correct', leaving only its (good) mutation-aliasing point against three misses. v5 actually surfaced the exact mechanism — including the double-slash garbage path and a verified production call-site check — but misclassified it as a test-setup inconsistency ('not a production bug') and missed the same guard in the other two files. v5's two test-coverage absence claims and v4's 'all USR_LOCAL_BIN usages covered' claim were all verified true, so neither candidate fabricated absences. Overall v5 is clearly stronger: more verified substance, partial credit on the key defect, fewer misses.
